seed_instruction: Write an email to my boss telling him that I am quitting.
org_constraint: Tailor the email for a professional audience, avoiding jargon and ensuring clarity.
new_constraint: Write the email in a humorous and witty tone.
instruction: Write an email to my boss telling him that I am quitting. Tailor the email for a professional audience, avoiding jargon and ensuring clarity. Write the email in a humorous and witty tone.
response:
Subject: A bittersweet announcement (and my formal resignation)

Dear [Boss's Name],

They say that all good things must come to an end, and unfortunately, my tenure here is one of those things. Please accept this email as formal notification that I am resigning from my position as [Job Title], with my final day being [Date].

I’ve spent a significant amount of time here, and while I’ve truly enjoyed our time working together, I’ve decided it’s time for me to embark on a new adventure. Think of it as a "career pivot"—or, in layman's terms, I’m moving on to a new challenge.

I want to thank you for the opportunity to work under your leadership. I’ve learned a great deal, survived many meetings that could have been emails, and managed to keep my caffeine intake at a level that was (mostly) sustainable. I truly appreciate the guidance and support you’ve provided during my time here.

Over the next [Number of weeks] weeks, I am fully committed to ensuring a seamless transition. I will do my best to wrap up my current projects and train my successor so thoroughly that they won't have to call me in a panic the moment I’m gone (though I can’t promise I won't answer the phone if the question is truly existential).

I’d love to stay in touch as I move on to my next chapter. You can reach me at [Personal Email Address] or on LinkedIn.

Thank you again for everything. I wish you and the team nothing but continued success—and slightly fewer "urgent" Friday afternoon requests.

Best regards,

[Your Name]<turn|>
